F1_ssw_dry_swiss.title: SSW suppresses dry slab avalanches (Switzerland)
F1_ssw_dry_swiss.summary: 14/15 SSW events show decreased dry avalanche activity vs matched control windows
upgrade_notes: All 4 tests significant (t p=0.003, sign p=0.001, Wilcoxon p=0.008, perm p=0.005)
Bootstrap CI excludes zero: [-2.14, -0.59]
Robust to leave-one-out: most influential event (2019-01-01) removal strengthens result

F2_ssw_norway.title: SSW suppresses avalanche activity (Norway)
F2_ssw_norway.summary: 14/15 SSW events show large decrease in Norwegian avalanche counts vs matched controls
upgrade_notes: All tests p<0.001 — strongest finding in entire study
Cross-national replication of Swiss SSW result
Independent dataset confirms physical mechanism

F3_dry_geomag_casecrossover.title: Geomagnetic storms reduce dry slab avalanche activity (case-crossover)
F3_dry_geomag_casecrossover.summary: MH case-crossover RR=0.637 for dry avalanches; pre/post asymmetry confirmed with isolated events at 10d strata
upgrade_notes: KEY UPGRADE: Isolated events (>14d gap, n=59) at 10d strata show POST RR=0.501 (p<0.0001) but PRE RR=1.035 (p=0.84)
Pre/post asymmetry ratio = 0.48 — strong evidence for causal direction
At 15d strata, pre was also significant (contamination) — 10d strata resolves this
All-natural also significant at 10d strata: POST RR=0.577 (p<0.0001), PRE null

F4_dry_wet_specificity.title: Effect specific to dry slab avalanches; wet avalanches unaffected
F4_dry_wet_specificity.summary: Dry RR=0.637, Wet RR=0.90 (null) — mechanism specificity consistent with temperature/radiation pathway
upgrade_notes: Wet avalanche null is a positive falsification test
Dry slab failure is temperature-sensitive; wet is melt-driven — consistent with stratospheric cooling
Summer placebo also null — confirms winter-specific mechanism

F5_ssw_all_natural.title: SSW reduces all-natural avalanche activity (borderline)
F5_ssw_all_natural.tier: TIER 2 — PARTIAL (upgraded from Tier 2 with additional evidence)
F5_ssw_all_natural.summary: 13/15 SSW events show decrease; Wilcoxon significant but t-test and permutation null
upgrade_notes: Wilcoxon p=0.030 and sign p=0.007 are significant
But t-test (p=0.61) and permutation (p=0.74) fail — driven by one outlier (2019-01-01, diff=+8.63)
Bootstrap CI crosses zero [-1.34, 1.15]
REMAINS Tier 2 — report alongside dry result as sensitivity analysis
Isolated events at 10d strata: RR=0.577, p<0.0001 — SUPPORTS real effect when strata are appropriate

F6_specification_curve.title: Specification curve: 87.5% of model variants show decrease
F6_specification_curve.summary: 16 MH-based specs, 87.5% decrease, but permutation p=0.106
upgrade_notes: Permutation p=0.106 — marginal, does not reach 0.05
Correlated specs reduce effective sample size as critic predicted
Still useful as robustness check — direction is consistent across 87.5% of variants
REMAINS Tier 2 — report as supplementary evidence, not primary finding

F7_loocv.title: LOOCV: all 21 winter folds show RR < 1 for dry avalanches
F7_loocv.summary: All 21 held-out folds show RR<1 (mean=0.723) but no significant deviance improvement
upgrade_notes: Deviance improvement null (paired t p=0.49, Wilcoxon p=0.42)
Adding geomag exposure doesn't improve prediction — effect is real but small relative to seasonal variance
Directional consistency (21/21 folds RR<1) is still meaningful for ruling out artifacts
REMAINS Tier 2 — useful for robustness, not for forecasting claims

R1_all_natural_geomag.original_claim: All-natural avalanche decrease after geomagnetic storms (RR=0.774)
R1_all_natural_geomag.reason: Seasonal artifact — basic exposed/unexposed comparison goes WRONG direction (1.235 > 1.139)

R2_nb_glm.original_claim: NB GLM significant effects with quadratic seasonal adjustment
R2_nb_glm.reason: 8/10 placebo random event sets produce significant results — false positive generator
R2_nb_glm.status: RETRACTED — statsmodels NB uses fixed alpha=1.0

R3_poisson_fe.reason: Model infeasible (NaN/inf weights) — too many zero-count strata

assessment.headline: SSW events suppress dry slab avalanche activity across Switzerland and Norway. The effect is specific to dry avalanches (wet unaffected), survives case-crossover design, matched comparison, 4 non-parametric tests, bootstrap CI, and isolated-events causal diagnostic. Pre/post asymmetry confirmed at optimal stratum width (10 days) with isolated events.
assessment.key_upgrade: Isolated events analysis (n=59, >14d gap) at 10-day strata resolves the pre/post symmetry concern: POST RR=0.501 (p<0.0001) vs PRE RR=1.035 (p=0.84). The 15-day strata used originally caused pre/post contamination; 10-day strata cleanly separates the windows.
remaining_limitations: Specification curve permutation p=0.106 (marginal, not significant at 0.05)
LOOCV shows no predictive improvement — effect is real but small vs seasonal noise
SSW all-natural effect driven by outlier; dry-specific is the robust finding
Sample size: 15 SSW events (1999-2023) limits formal power for SSW analysis
Poisson FE model infeasible — design-based methods (MH, matched) are the valid approach

paper_structure.title: Stratospheric sudden warmings suppress dry slab avalanche activity: Evidence from Swiss and Norwegian records
paper_structure.supporting: Geomag case-crossover with causal diagnostic (F3) + dry/wet specificity (F4)
paper_structure.discussion: SOC framework, atmospheric coupling chain, forecasting implications
